# Issue 7.2: Animal Profile & Sighting Timeline

## Objective
Detail an individual animal's life story, discoverer, and subsequent visitor sightings.

## Tasks
- [ ] Build a full-screen drawer or modal view when an animal is clicked from the map or journal.
- [ ] Display the generated AI backstory.
- [ ] Include a timeline of all `encounters` displaying:
  - Encounter date & time
  - Photo uploaded during that encounter
  - Username of the player who encountered it
- [ ] Style user profiles and timeline elements cleanly.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Sighting timeline displays multiple historical encounters.
- [ ] Details show creator's and subsequent visitor's cached Clerk names.

## Labels

# Issue 6.1: Web Camera & Capture UI Component

## Objective
Build a responsive camera viewfinder that queries the browser's geolocation API and captures images.

## Tasks
- [ ] Implement HTML5 camera interface using `navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia` or fallback file upload input.
- [ ] Capture photos as Blob/Base64 data.

- [ ] Build UI showing loading overlays during Gemini evaluation and result states:

  - "Say hello" / encounter card for existing animal encounters.
- [ ] Add style and layout to match the premium dark mode / glassmorphism aesthetic guidelines.

body:
# Issue 5.3: Core Capture Coordinator Endpoint (/api/capture)

## Objective
Chain the photo capture, verification, matching, and database persistence into a single transactional serverless API endpoint.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create route handler for `POST /api/capture`.
- [ ] Parse multipart form data containing the image stream and latitude/longitude inputs.
- [ ] Execute capture pipeline:
  - Call Gemini API to verify the image. If not an animal, return an error.
  - Upload the image to Vercel Blob.
  - Compute the H3 index.
  - Call the Proximity Matching service.
  - Execute database transactions:
    - If New Discovery: Create `animals` record and create matching `encounters` entry.
    - If Encounter: Create `encounters` record linked to the existing `animals` record.
- [ ] Return details back to client (isNewDiscovery, animal details, encounter details).
- [ ] Write integration tests for the full pipeline.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `POST /api/capture` endpoint processes upload, matching, and DB persistence.
- [ ] End-to-end integration tests execute successfully.

## Labels
- `api`
- `phase-5`
- `planned`

labels.name: planned
labels.description: Issue has been planned and is ready for implementation

# Issue 5.2: Gemini Vision API Integration

## Objective
Send captured images to Gemini to verify if they contain an animal, extract the species, and generate creative backstories.

## Tasks
- [ ] Design structured JSON response schemas for Gemini to enforce consistency:
  - `isAnimal: boolean`
  - `species: string` (e.g. "squirrel", "goose", "rabbit", etc.)
  - `name: string` (e.g. "Charles")
  - `backstory: string`
- [ ] Implement backend service calling Gemini's multimodal models with standard prompt parameters.
- [ ] Parse and validate Gemini responses against the schema.
- [ ] Add unit/integration tests with mocked image buffers and sample outputs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] API integration returns structured JSON output from Gemini.
- [ ] Non-animal images are rejected with `isAnimal: false` cleanly.
- [ ] Integration tests verify the parsing logic.

## Labels
- `ai`
- `phase-5`
- `planned`

body:
# Issue 4.2: Proximity Matching Engine

## Objective
Build the matching engine layer that handles species capacity and proximity detection based on Uber H3 resolution 9 cells.

## Tasks
- [ ] Create `/services/matching/types.ts` to house the data models: `MatchingResult`, `MatchingParams`, and `AnimalMatchingStrategy`.
- [ ] Create `/services/matching/config.ts` containing carrying-capacity saturation configs per species (defaulting to 3).
- [ ] Implement `/services/matching/proximity.ts` (implementing `AnimalMatchingStrategy`):
  - Query center cell + 6 neighbors for existing animals of the target species.
  - If under saturation capacity: return `{ isNewDiscovery: true, matchedAnimal: null }`.
  - If at/above saturation: calculate Euclidean or Haversine distance to find the closest existing discovery and return `{ isNewDiscovery: false, matchedAnimal: closest }`.
- [ ] Implement `/services/matching/index.ts` as the entry service wrapper.
- [ ] Add unit tests in `/tests/services/matching.test.ts` covering saturation boundaries, neighborhood checks, and minimum distance calculations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Service returns `isNewDiscovery: true` when count of species in the 7-cell neighborhood is below limit.
- [ ] Service returns the closest animal when count of species in the 7-cell neighborhood is at or above capacity.
- [ ] Unit tests verify matching logic boundaries.

## Labels
- `location`
- `phase-4`
- `planned`

labels.name: planned
labels.description: Issue has been planned and is ready for implementation

# Issue 4.1: Spatial Indexing Service (h3-js)

## Objective
Implement H3 hexagonal location mapping functions to support geospatial partitioning.

## Tasks
- [ ] Install `h3-js` library.
- [ ] Create `/services/location/h3.ts` to compute resolution 9 cell index from latitude and longitude coordinates.
- [ ] Implement a method in `/services/location/h3.ts` to return the 7-cell cluster (current center cell + 6 adjacent neighbors using `gridDisk` or equivalent `h3-js` function) representing a user's local zone.
- [ ] Write unit tests in `/tests/services/location.test.ts` verifying correct conversions and boundary behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Correct resolution 9 indexes are generated from GPS coordinates.
- [ ] 7-cell local zone clusters are correctly resolved.
- [ ] Unit tests pass and verify conversion correctness.

## Labels
- `location`
- `phase-4`
- `planned`

# Issue 3.2: Clerk Authentication & User Cache Synchronization

## Objective
Integrate Clerk auth into the Next.js app and implement the lazy-sync mechanism to save active profiles in Neon.

## Tasks
- [ ] Install Clerk SDK `@clerk/nextjs`.
- [ ] Set up Clerk provider in `app/layout.tsx` and configure auth middleware.
- [ ] Implement a profile-sync helper `/lib/auth/sync.ts`:
  - Checks if the current authenticated `userId` exists in the Neon `users` table.
  - If not, fetches user info from Clerk Node SDK (`clerkClient.users.getUser`) and inserts it into the database.
- [ ] Set up public vs protected routes (e.g. landing page is public, capture/journal is protected).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Protected routes redirect unauthenticated users to Clerk signup/login.
- [ ] Upon first login, user profile is successfully cached/synchronized in the local Neon database.

## Labels
- `auth`
- `boilerplate`
- `phase-3`

body:
# Issue 3.1: Database Schema & Connection Pool Setup (Neon + Drizzle)

## Objective
Create the core tables and wire up Drizzle ORM to work with Neon serverless connections.

## Tasks
- [ ] Install `drizzle-orm`, `drizzle-kit`, and `@neondatabase/serverless`.
- [ ] Create the schema in `/lib/db/schema.ts` defining:
  - `users`: Clerk cached profiles (ID, username, avatar_url, created_at)
  - `animals`: Unique discoveries (ID, name, species, backstory, photo_url, discoverer_id, h3_index [len 32], latitude, longitude, created_at)
  - `encounters`: User sightings (ID, user_id, animal_id, photo_url, created_at)
- [ ] Set up connection pool and client in `/lib/db/index.ts` utilizing serverless web sockets.
- [ ] Configure `drizzle.config.ts` and set up migration scripts in `package.json`:
  - `db:generate` (generate SQL migrations)
  - `db:migrate` (apply migrations to database)
  - `db:studio` (launch Drizzle DBM UI)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Migration files generated successfully.
- [ ] Database connection works and can apply migrations to local/Neon database.

## Labels
- `database`
- `boilerplate`
- `phase-3`

# Issue 2.2: API Clients & Mock Wrappers Boilerplate

## Objective
Install standard SDKs and build clean API client wrappers that fall back gracefully or log errors if environment keys are missing.

## Tasks
- [ ] Install `@google/generative-ai` SDK.
- [ ] Create `/services/gemini/client.ts` with structured initialization and a robust safety wrapper for calling Gemini.
- [ ] Create standard mock payloads in `/services/gemini/mocks.ts` simulating:
  - Successful animal identification (e.g., standard "squirrel", "duck" JSON payloads)
  - Rejected identification ("not an animal" responses)
  - Network failure stubs for tests and local development without consuming quota.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] SDK initialization handles missing API keys gracefully (logs warning in dev/test, doesn't crash).
- [ ] Mock responses are accessible and correctly structured per the PRD JSON model.

## Labels
- `boilerplate`
- `ai`
- `phase-2`
